name: Dropbox Video Download MCP Server
what_it_does: MCP server for connecting video workflows to Dropbox cloud storage. Enables AI agents to organize and access video assets stored in Dropbox — downloading videos, managing video file organization, and integrating Dropbox video storage into AI-driven media processing workflows.
use_cases: Accessing and downloading video assets from Dropbox for AI video processing workflows
Managing video file organization in Dropbox from media management agents
Integrating Dropbox video storage into content processing automation pipelines
Building video workflow automation with Dropbox as the storage backend
not_for: Google Drive or OneDrive video storage (Dropbox-specific)
Large-scale video processing pipelines at scale
best_when: A video producer or content creator using Dropbox as their video asset storage wants AI agents to help manage, access, and process video files — enabling automation of video asset workflows.
avoid_when: You use other cloud storage providers. Also: large video files can consume significant Dropbox API bandwidth — consider costs for high-volume video processing.

auth.notes: Dropbox OAuth 2.0. Use minimal scopes — files.content.read for download access. Avoid write permissions unless needed.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Dropbox free tier for small storage; paid for more storage and bandwidth
pricing.paid_tiers: Dropbox Plus/Professional/Business for larger storage and bandwidth
pricing.notes: Dropbox free tier available with limits. Video files can consume storage quickly. MCP server is free open source.

performance.rate_limits: Dropbox API rate limits and bandwidth limits apply

known_agent_gotchas: PRIVACY: Dropbox video content may include personal or proprietary media
Large video file downloads consume Dropbox bandwidth — monitor for API limits
OAuth token refresh needed for long-running video processing agents
Video file access depends on Dropbox storage quota availability
Niche implementation — community maintained with limited documentation
agent_readiness.security_notes: PRIVACY: Video content may be personal or proprietary. OAuth with minimal scopes. HTTPS to Dropbox. Monitor for unauthorized access.

security_score_components.security_notes: Video content privacy. OAuth minimal scopes. Dropbox HTTPS. Monitor access.
